description: This file defines affirmative question formation in the Future Perfect tense in Njo Viri (Balanda). Future perfect affirmative questions ask whether an action will have been completed by a future time.

general_principle.structure: Future perfect affirmative questions in Balanda are formed by keeping the affirmative future perfect structure unchanged and adding the question marker 'we?' at the end of the sentence.

question_structure.notes: Used to ask whether an action will have been completed in the future. | The future prefix 'le-' must remain attached to the verb base; The verb must remain in base form; The completion marker 'dheki' must be retained; The question marker 'we?' must be sentence-final; Word order must not change

tense_and_aspect_distinction.structure: Future perfect affirmative questions encode future tense and completion.
tense_and_aspect_distinction.notes: 'le-' encodes future tense; 'dheki' encodes completed result

word_order_rules.notes: Subject; le-Verb; Object / Complement; dheki; we?

error_constraints.structure: Do not omit 'le-'; Do not omit 'dheki'; Do not use the progressive prefix 'ti-'; Do not use the copula 'ma'; Do not add the negation marker 'gba'; Do not move 'we?' from sentence-final position

model_behavior_guidelines.structure: {"enforce_le_prefix": true, "enforce_base_verb": true, "require_dheki": true, "keep_we_sentence_final": true, "block_other_tense_aspect_markers": true, "map_to_english_future_perfect_questions": true}

output: Will I have eaten fish?
Will she have drunk water?

input: Danda leteli muru famvara dheki we?
output: Will we have slept on the bed?
Will Uku have gone?
Will Ngande have come home?

input: Ndi lenonö gbi difo dheki we?
output: Will they have walked in the compound?
Will she have run fast?

input: Uku lemande gbi njü dheki we?
output: Will Uku have worked in the garden?

input: Wiki lemoliki gbi difo dheki we?
output: Will the child have played in the compound?
Will I have watched the dog?

input: Ne levele njo balanda dheki we?
output: Will I have spoken Balanda?
Will the girl have written words?
Will I have read a book?
Will he have thought about it?
Will they have known it?
Will I have asked them?

input: Ngande levele zi ndi dheki we?
output: Will Ngande have told them?

input: Wilii lekeye ti naa ni dheki we?
output: Will the girl have called her mother?
Will I have loved you?

input: Ni lemaguku ti kpörö dheki we?
output: Will he have feared the snake?
Will she have hated him?
Will Ngande have surprised him?
Will he have seemed calm?
Will the girl have tied the shoe?
Will he have cut the pot?
Will I have pulled the door?
Will we have pushed the box?
Will the girl have washed the shoe?
Will I have hidden the beads?
Will Ngande have built a house?
Will I have created words?
Will she have changed the words?
Will I have turned the door?
Will we have added money?
Will I have described Balanda?
Will they have helped the poor?
Will Ngande have initiated the project?
Will I have contributed to the project?
Will the girl have separated trash?
Will we have upheld the law?
Will I have wanted water?
Will she have given money?
Will I have found money?
Will Uku have tried the cloth?
Will Ngande have left home?
Will I have arrived in Wau?
Will he have sharpened the knife?
Will Ngande have imitated adults?
Will we have started the project?
Will he have fallen?
